text: Kamisato Ayaka is the younger sister of Kamisato Ayato.
Kamisato Ayato is the current head of the Kamisato Clan and leader of the Yashiro Commission.
The Kamisato Clan leads the Yashiro Commission, one of the Tri-Commissions that govern Inazuma.
Ayaka and Ayato lost both of their parents at a young age during the decline of the Kamisato Clan.
The Kamisato Clan once suffered political decline and loss of influence within the Inazuma Shogunate.
Ayaka serves as the public face of the Kamisato Clan and is admired across Inazuma as the Shirasagi Himegimi.
Ayaka manages social affairs, ceremonial duties, and public relations for the Kamisato Clan.
The Kamisato Clan oversees the Shuumatsuban, a secret ninja and intelligence organization in Inazuma.
Ayato and Ayaka deeply trust each other and share the burden of restoring the Kamisato Clan.
Kamisato Kayo, Ayaka's mother, was a graceful and elegant woman who managed the affairs of the Kamisato Clan with calm composure and kindness.
Ayaka viewed her mother Kayo as the embodiment of perfection due to her elegance, serenity, and unwavering smile.
After her mother's death, Ayaka realized she could no longer remain a sheltered child and had to mature quickly to support the Kamisato Clan.
Kayo recorded her private thoughts and unfulfilled wishes under the name 'Tsubaki.'
Ayaka once believed Tsubaki was her mother's close friend until she later discovered that Tsubaki was actually a hidden side of Kayo herself.
Through learning about Tsubaki, Ayaka realized that beneath her noble responsibilities, she also possessed the heart of an ordinary young woman.
Ayaka befriended a blind elderly street musician who once taught her how to make and play a wooden zither.
Ayaka secretly visits the blind old woman regularly and brings her daily necessities while pretending to be an ordinary local resident.
The blind old woman's kindness reminded Ayaka of her late parents.
Despite being a noblewoman, Ayaka deeply empathizes with ordinary people and quietly helps those in need.
